title: eremophila interstans subsp. virgata
intro: Eremophila interstans subsp. virgata, also known as the native emu bush, is a flowering plant native to Western Australia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of the plant include its ability to soothe and calm the skin, as well as its potential to reduce fever and relieve respiratory issues. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions and promote overall well-being.
